11 activity highlights this cycle 4 regions reporting · Week of May 5–14, 2026 Submitted by the global marketing team via Asana

Global Marketing Activity Hub.

Trent Raleigh

Hammer Strength team shows up strong at PFIFC

Reid, Jessica, Matt and Trent anchored the booth with the Heritage Edition rig, working the floor across three days of operator conversations and partnership momentum.

EventMay 12 North America

The North America team represented Life Fitness and Hammer Strength at PFIFC, with Reid, Jessica, Matt and Trent staffing the booth across all three show days. The Heritage Edition rig anchored the space and pulled steady traffic from operators.

The team logged a strong run of qualified conversations, several tied to the Planet Fitness partnership story featured on the booth backwall. A handful of operators asked for follow-up reference visits. Worth sharing with the wider team as a model for how to staff and stage a partner conference.

Trent Raleigh

Connecting with an industry icon at HFA

Trent and the team caught up with a longtime industry figure on the HFA show floor, the kind of relationship-building that keeps Hammer Strength close to the operators who shaped the category.

EventMay 7 North America

On the HFA show floor, Trent and the North America team spent time with a longtime industry figure whose history with the category runs deep. Moments like this do not show up in a pipeline report, but they keep the brand connected to the people who built the operator landscape.

Shared as a reminder that field presence is relationship work as much as lead work.

Marcus Lee

Technogym lands a flagship Sydney account, here's what we heard

The APAC team flagged a competitive loss worth learning from. Technogym won a high-profile Sydney club on a bundled service package. Notes on their pitch are attached for the broader team.

Field IntelMay 8 APAC

Marcus shared a competitive story from the Australian market: Technogym won a high-profile Sydney club, reportedly on a bundled equipment-plus-service package rather than on product alone. The APAC team gathered notes on how the deal was positioned.

Not a win for us, but a useful read on how a key competitor is packaging in the region. Notes are attached so other markets can pressure-test their own service positioning.

Lena Brandt

Germany's Q2 paid social campaign clears benchmark by 60%

The DACH team's spring paid social push beat the regional cost-per-lead benchmark by 60%, driven by a fully localized creative set and tighter operator targeting.

CampaignMay 9 EMEA

The Germany team's Q2 paid social campaign closed out 60% ahead of the EMEA cost-per-lead benchmark. Lena credits a fully localized creative set, built specifically for German operators rather than adapted from global assets, plus tighter audience targeting by club type.

The team has offered to share the creative framework and targeting approach with other EMEA markets. A short writeup is attached for anyone who wants to run a similar test.

Jordan Pierce

US paid search refresh cuts cost-per-lead

A rebuilt paid search structure and tighter keyword grouping brought cost-per-lead down meaningfully across the North America accounts.

CampaignMay 13 North America

The North America team rebuilt its paid search account structure with tighter keyword grouping and refreshed ad copy. Cost-per-lead came down meaningfully in the first few weeks, with no drop in lead quality.

Jordan plans to document the new structure so other regions can replicate it.

Priya Shah

UK lifecycle email refresh lifts open rates 14 points

The UK team rebuilt its operator lifecycle emails around a problem-first structure. Early results show open rates up 14 points and the strongest click-through the program has seen.

CampaignMay 10 EMEA

The UK team rebuilt its operator lifecycle email program around a problem-first structure, leading with the operator challenge rather than the product. The first full cycle is in, and open rates are up 14 points with the strongest click-through the program has recorded.

Priya has shared the new sequence structure with the wider EMEA email group as a reference.

Melissa Cohen

Chicago marketing team trades the office for a volunteer day

The Rosemont crew spent a morning packing meals at a local food bank. A good reset, and a reminder of why team culture shows up in the work.

TeamMay 6 North America

The Rosemont marketing team spent a morning volunteering at a local food bank, packing meals as a group. Melissa shared it as a team-culture moment worth seeing alongside the campaign and event updates.

Culture is part of the regional story too, and these are the moments that do not always make it into a status report.

Antoine Mercier

France distributor open house draws a full room

The France team supported a distributor open house in Lyon with strong operator turnout and several warm follow-ups.

EventMay 6 EMEA

The France team supported a distributor open house in Lyon. Operator turnout filled the room, and the team came away with several warm follow-up conversations to work over the coming weeks.

Erik Lindqvist

A Nordic team's booth concept gets picked up region-wide

The booth layout the Nordics team designed for a regional expo is being adopted as the EMEA template, a nice example of one market's work scaling across the region.

TeamMay 11 EMEA

The booth concept the Nordics team built for a regional expo, layout, traffic flow and demo staging, is being adopted as the standard EMEA template. A good example of one market's work being recognized and scaled across the region.

Erik shared the layout files and a short rationale doc with the EMEA events group.

Camila Rocha

Brazil launches a localized social series for independent operators

A new short-form content series built for Brazil's independent gym segment is live, with strong early engagement.

CampaignMay 5 LATAM

The Brazil team launched a short-form social content series tailored to the country's large independent gym segment. Early engagement is running well above the regional baseline.

Camila plans to share the content framework with the wider LATAM team once the first run wraps.

Wei Tan

Singapore team on-site for a flagship club opening

The APAC team supported a flagship club opening in person, capturing content and operator testimonials for regional use.

EventMay 9 APAC

The Singapore team was on-site for a flagship club opening, capturing photo and video content plus operator testimonials. The material is being packaged for use across APAC channels.
